logging(3) Library Functions Manual logging(3)

NAME

logging - Logging System


- 📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

SYNOPSIS

Files


file apple.c
Apple unified logging (os_log) integration. file colorize.c
Log message colorization for terminal output. file format.c
Log format parser implementation. file json.c
📝 JSON structured logging output using yyjson file log.c
📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output file mmap.c
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging implementation. file named.c
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file raylib.c
Raylib logging integration. file wasm.c
WASM logging support for routing to browser console. file colorize.h
Log message colorization for terminal output. file json.h
📝 JSON structured logging output file log.h
📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control file mmap.h
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging with crash safety. file named.h
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file types.h
Log level types and constants.

A single buffered log entry.

Default log level for debug builds (DEBUG and above). #define MAX_LOG_SIZE (3 * 1024 * 1024)
Maximum log file size in bytes (3MB) before rotation. #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_SIZE (64 * 1024)
Maximum size of terminal output buffer (64KB). #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_ENTRIES 256
Maximum number of buffered log entries. #define LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 4096
Maximum size of a single log message (including formatting). #define LOG_MMAP_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 1024
Maximum size of a log message in mmap mode. #define LOG_HEADER_BUFFER_SIZE 512
Maximum size of a log header (timestamp, level, file:line:func). #define LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE 32
Maximum size of a timestamp string. #define log_plain(...)
Plain logging - writes to both log file and stderr without timestamps or log levels. #define log_plain_stderr(...)
Plain logging to stderr with newline. #define log_plain_stdout(...)
Plain logging to stdout with newline. #define log_plain_stderr_nonewline(...)
Plain logging to stderr without newline - for interactive prompts. #define log_file(...)
File-only logging - writes to log file only, no stderr output. #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)
Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)
Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). #define log_once(log_level, fmt, ...)
Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). #define LOG_CLIENT_IMPL(client, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends DEBUG log message to client. #define log_info_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends INFO log message to client. #define log_warn_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends WARN log message to client. #define log_error_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends ERROR log message to client. #define log_fatal_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends FATAL log message to client. #define LOG_SERVER_IMPL(sockfd, crypto_ctx, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends DEBUG log message to server. #define log_info_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends INFO log message to server. #define log_warn_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends WARN log message to server. #define log_error_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends ERROR log message to server. #define log_fatal_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends FATAL log message to server.

Enumerations

Logging levels enumeration. enum remote_log_direction { REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN = 0, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT = 1, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER = 2 }
Remote log packet direction enumeration.

Logging Macros

Note

Compile-time log level stripping: In release builds, log_dev() and log_debug() are compiled out completely (no runtime overhead). Override with LOG_COMPILE_LEVEL.


#define log_dev(...)
Log a DEV message (most verbose, development only). #define log_debug(...)
Log a DEBUG message. #define log_info(...)
Log an INFO message. #define log_warn(...)
Log a WARN message. #define log_error(...)
Log an ERROR message. #define log_fatal(...)
Log a FATAL message.

Detailed Description

📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

This header provides a comprehensive logging system with:

  • Multiple log levels (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
  • File and terminal output with automatic color coding
  • Terminal capability detection (16-color, 256-color, truecolor)
  • Rate-limited logging macros for high-frequency logging
  • Thread-safe logging operations
  • Automatic log file rotation when size limit is reached

Note

In debug builds, log macros include file/line/function information. In release builds, this information is omitted.

This module formats named objects in log messages by scanning for hex addresses and replacing them with friendly descriptions from the named object registry when available.

For example:

  • Input: "Locked 0x7f1234567890 successfully"
  • Output: "Locked mutex: recv_mutex.2 (0x7f1234567890) successfully"

When an address is not registered, it's left unchanged.

  LOG_CLIENT_IMPL( client, level, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  if ((client)->crypto_initialized) {                                                                                \


    const struct crypto_context_t *_ctx = crypto_handshake_get_context(&(client)->crypto_handshake_ctx);             \


    log_net_message((client)->socket, _ctx, level, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \


                    __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                   \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)

#define
  log_every( log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \


  uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \


  uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \


  if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \


    if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \


      log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \


    }                                                                                                                \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). Logs at most once per
    specified time interval. Useful for threads that have an FPS and functions
    they call to prevent spammy logs.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


interval_us Minimum microseconds between log messages


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Each call site maintains its own static atomic timer, so
  different call sites can log independently. Thread-safe via atomic
  compare-exchange.



Uses platform_get_monotonic_time_us() for cross-platform
    time.




Definition at line 681 of file log/log.h.


681 #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)                                                                    \
682   do {                                                                                                                 \
683     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \
684     uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \
685     uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \
686     if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \
687       if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \
688         log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \
689       }                                                                                                                \
690     }                                                                                                                  \
691   } while (0)

#define
  log_nth( log_level, n, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \


  uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \


  uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \


  atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \


  if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). Logs a
    message every nth time the code is executed. Useful for logging periodic
    events in tight loops without spamming the log. For example, log_nth(INFO,
    1000, "Processed items") logs every 1000 calls.




Each call site maintains its own static counter, so different call
    sites can log independently at different frequencies.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


n Log every nth call (1 = every call, 2 = every 2nd call, etc.)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic fetch_add.


Definition at line 749 of file log/log.h.


749 #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)                                                                                \
750   do {                                                                                                                 \
751     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \
752     uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \
753     uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \
754     atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \
755     if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \
756       log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \
757     }                                                                                                                  \
758   } while (0)










#define
  log_once( log_level, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_once_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                            \


  uint64_t _log_once_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_once_counter);                         \


  if (_log_once_count == 0) {                                                                                        \


    atomic_store_u64(&_log_once_counter, 1);                                              \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). Logs a message
    exactly once, no matter how many times the code is executed. Each call site
    maintains its own static counter, so different call sites can log
    independently.




Useful for one-time initialization messages, warnings, or debug
    output that should only appear once per session.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic operations.

enum log_color_t


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Color enum for logging - indexes into color arrays. These values
    directly index into level_colors arrays. Order matches DEV, DEBUG, WARN,
    INFO, ERROR, FATAL, GREY, RESET.


Enumerator
  





  
LOG_COLOR_DEV
    

  
Blue - DEV messages

  
LOG_COLOR_DEBUG
    

  
Cyan - DEBUG messages

  
LOG_COLOR_INFO
    

  
Green - INFO messages

  
LOG_COLOR_WARN
    

  
Yellow - WARN messages

  
LOG_COLOR_ERROR
    

  
Red - ERROR messages

  
LOG_COLOR_FATAL
    

  
Magenta - FATAL messages

  
LOG_COLOR_GREY
    

  
Grey - for neutral messages or labels

  
LOG_COLOR_RESET
    

  
Reset to default

Function
  Documentation




const
  char * colorize_named_string (const char * name_str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Colorize a "type/name" string for display.


Parameters


name_str String in "type/name" or
  "type/name.id" format


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes:
  type(yellow)/name(blue)


Used by memory report and other displays to colorize named
    objects. Applies colors:


    
  
  • Type: yellow (LOG_COLOR_WARN)
    • 
  
  • Name: blue (LOG_COLOR_DEV)
    • 
  
  • Separator: uncolored
    • 



Example: "thread/splash_anim.0" displays with type in
    yellow, name in blue




Note


Returns pointer to rotating static buffer (4 buffers)



Safe to use in printf-style calls: "%s"




Colorize a "type/name" string for display.




Parameters


name_str String in "type/name
  (0xaddress)" format (e.g., "thread/splash_anim.0
  (0x7f123456)")


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes: type(yellow)/name(blue)
  (id(grey))


Used by memory report to colorize named object display. Returns a
    pointer to a static rotating buffer (4 buffers, valid until next call).




Example: "thread/splash_anim.0 (0x7f123456)" displays
    with:


    
  
  • "thread" in yellow
    • 
  
  • "splash_anim.0" in blue
    • 
  
  • "0x7f123456" in grey
    • 



Definition at line 976 of file log/named.c.


976                                                         {
977 #define COLORIZE_BUFFERS 4
978 #define COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE 512
979   static char buffers[COLORIZE_BUFFERS][COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE];
980   static int buffer_idx = 0;
981 
982   if (!name_str || name_str[0] == '\0') {
983     return name_str;
984   }
985 
986   char *current_buf = buffers[buffer_idx];
987   buffer_idx = (buffer_idx + 1) % COLORIZE_BUFFERS;
988 
989   /* Find the slash separator (type/name) */
990   const char *slash = strchr(name_str, '/');
991   if (!slash) {
992     /* No slash found - just return the string as-is */
993     return name_str;
994   }
995 
996   /* Find opening paren for address */
997   const char *paren = strchr(name_str, '(');
998 
999   /* Extract type (before slash) */
1000   size_t type_len = slash - name_str;
1001   char type_buf[256];
1002   size_t copy_len = type_len < sizeof(type_buf) - 1 ? type_len : sizeof(type_buf) - 1;
1003   memcpy(type_buf, name_str, copy_len);
1004   type_buf[copy_len] = '\0';
1005 
1006   /* Extract name (between slash and paren, or end of string) */
1007   const char *name_start = slash + 1;
1008   size_t name_len;
1009   if (paren && paren > slash) {
1010     /* Skip spaces before paren */
1011     const char *name_end = paren - 1;
1012     while (name_end > name_start && *name_end == ' ') {
1013       name_end--;
1014     }
1015     name_len = name_end - name_start + 1;
1016   } else {
1017     name_len = strlen(name_start);
1018   }
1019 
1020   char name_buf[256];
1021   size_t name_copy_len = name_len < sizeof(name_buf) - 1 ? name_len : sizeof(name_buf) - 1;
1022   memcpy(name_buf, name_start, name_copy_len);
1023   name_buf[name_copy_len] = '\0';
1024 
1025   /* Apply colors */
1026   const char *type_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, type_buf);
1027   const char *name_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, name_buf);
1028 
1029   /* Format: type(yellow)/name(blue) and add address part if present */
1030   if (paren) {
1031     /* Extract address (0x...) from parentheses */
1032     const char *addr_start = paren + 1;
1033     const char *addr_end = strchr(addr_start, ')');
1034     if (addr_end) {
1035       size_t addr_len = addr_end - addr_start;
1036       char addr_buf[128];
1037       size_t addr_copy_len = addr_len < sizeof(addr_buf) - 1 ? addr_len : sizeof(addr_buf) - 1;
1038       memcpy(addr_buf, addr_start, addr_copy_len);
1039       addr_buf[addr_copy_len] = '\0';
1040 
1041       const char *addr_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, addr_buf);
1042       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s (%s)", type_colored, name_colored, addr_colored);
1043     } else {
1044       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s %s", type_colored, name_colored, paren);
1045     }
1046   } else {
1047     safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s", type_colored, name_colored);
1048   }
1049 
1050   return current_buf;
1051 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFERS
1052 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE
1053 }






References colored_string(), COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE,
    COLORIZE_BUFFERS, LOG_COLOR_DEV, LOG_COLOR_GREY,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, and safe_snprintf().

  * format_message (const char * format, va_list args)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Format a message using va_list.


Parameters


format Format string


args Variable arguments list


Returns


Formatted message string (must be freed by caller)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get current time as formatted string.


Parameters


time_buf Output buffer for formatted time


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null
  terminator)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Destroy the logging system and close log file.


Definition at line 627 of file log/log.c.


627                        {
628   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
629   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
630     log_mmap_destroy();
631   }
632 
633   // Cleanup grep filter
634   grep_destroy();
635 
636   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
637    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
638    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
639    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
640   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
641 
642   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
643   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
644   if (g_log.format) {
645     log_template_free(g_log.format);
646     g_log.format = NULL;
647   }
648   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
649     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
650     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
651   }
652   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
653 
654   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
655   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
656   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
657     platform_close(old_file);
658   }
659   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
660 
661   // Destroy rotation mutex
662   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
663 }






References atomic_load_int(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_destroy(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), log_mmap_destroy(),
    log_mmap_is_active(), log_template_free(), and

  log_disable_file_output (void )

asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap (const char *
  log_path)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap-based logging. When enabled, log messages
    bypass the mutex and use atomic operations to write directly to a
    memory-mapped log file as human-readable text.




Benefits:


    
  
  • No mutex contention between logging threads
    • 
  
  • Crash-safe: text is written directly to mmap'd file, readable after
    crash
    • 
  
  • Fast path uses atomic fetch_add, no locks
    • 
  
  • ERROR/FATAL messages sync immediately for visibility
    • 
  
  • Simple: log file IS the mmap file (no separate binary format)
    • 



Parameters


log_path Path to the log file (will be
  memory-mapped)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Call log_init() first, then
  log_enable_mmap() to upgrade to lock-free


Definition at line 1775 of file log/log.c.


1775                                                         {
1776   return log_enable_mmap_sized(log_path, 0); /* Use default size */
1777 }

Get the current log format template (opaque pointer).


Returns


Opaque pointer to the current log format template (may be
  NULL if not set) Cast to log_template_t* after including
  log/format.h


Returns the compiled log format template used by the logging
    system. This is useful for code that needs to format log entries using the
    same template as the rest of the logging system (e.g., platform code).

  the logging system



It's safe to call before log_init() (will return NULL)




Return type is void* (opaque) to avoid circular dependency with
    format.h

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Initialize the logging system.


Parameters


filename Log file path (or NULL for no file
  logging)


level Minimum log level to output


force_stderr If true, route ALL logs to stderr (for client mode to keep
  stdout clean)


use_mmap If true, use fully lock-free mmap logging (recommended). If mmap
  fails, uses stderr only (no mutex fallback).


Note


When use_mmap=true, the entire logging path is lock-free:

    
  
  • File output uses atomic operations on mmap'd memory
    • 
  
  • Terminal output uses atomic fprintf/fwrite to fd
    • 
  
  • No mutex is ever acquired in the hot path
    • 





Definition at line 533 of file log/log.c.


533                                                                                          {
534 
535   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
536   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
537   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
538   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
539 
540   // Set basic config using atomic stores
541   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
542   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
543 
544   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
545   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
546   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
547   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
548     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
549   }
550 
551   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
552   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
553   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
554     platform_close(old_file);
555     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
556   }
557 
558   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
559   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
560   if (env_level_str) {
561     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
562   } else {
563     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
564   }
565 
566   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
567   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
568 
569   if (filename) {
570     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
571 
572     if (use_mmap) {
573       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
574       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
575       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
576         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
577       } else {
578         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
579         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
580           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
581         }
582         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
583         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
584       }
585     } else {
586       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
587       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
588       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
589       if (fd < 0) {
590         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
591           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
592         }
593         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
594       }
595     }
596   } else {
597     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
598     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
599   }
600 
601   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
602   log_set_format(NULL, false);
603 
604   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
605   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
606     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
607   }
608 
609   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
610 
611   // Reset terminal detection if needed
612   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
613     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
614   }
615 
616   // Detect terminal capabilities
617   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
618 
619   // NOTE: Color initialization happens separately via log_set_color_scheme()
620   // after options are parsed. Logging works without colors until then.
621   // NOTE: Grep filter initialization happens in main.c after options_init() completes.
622 
623   // Initialize raylib logging integration
624   log_init_raylib();
625 }

Initialize logging color system with current terminal

1677                            {
1678 
1679   /* Skip color initialization during terminal detection to avoid mutex deadlock */

1681     return;
1682   }
1683 
1684 
1685   /* Skip color initialization before logging is fully initialized */
1686   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
1687     return;
1688   }
1689 
1690 
1691   if (g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1692     return;
1693   }
1694 
1695 
1696   /* Get active color scheme - this ensures color system is initialized */
1697   const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
1698   if (!scheme) {
1699     /* Don't mark as initialized if we can't get a color scheme - return NULL instead */
1700     return;
1701   }
1702 
1703 
1704   /* Acquire mutex for compilation (mutex is now initialized by colorscheme_init) */
1705   mutex_lock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1706 
1707   /* Debug: Check if g_log.compiled_colors is actually zero-initialized */
1708   /* Zero the structure on first use to avoid freeing garbage pointers */
1709   /* (static = {0} produces garbage in this build for unknown reasons) */

1711   if (first_compile) {
1712     memset(&g_log.compiled_colors, 0, sizeof(g_log.compiled_colors));
1713     first_compile = false;
1714   }
1715 
1716   /* Detect terminal background */
1717   terminal_background_t background = detect_terminal_background();
1718   /* Determine color mode for compilation */
1719   terminal_color_mode_t mode;
1720   if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
1721     mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
1722   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1723     mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
1724   } else {
1725     mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
1726   }
1727   /* Compile the color scheme to ANSI codes */
1728   asciichat_error_t result = colorscheme_compile_scheme(scheme, mode, background, &g_log.compiled_colors);
1729   g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized = true;
1730   mutex_unlock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1731 
1732   /* Log outside of mutex lock to avoid recursive lock deadlock */
1733   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1734     log_debug("Failed to compile color scheme: %d", result);
1735   }
1736 }

bool log_lock_terminal (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Lock terminal output for exclusive access by the calling thread.
    Call this before interactive prompts (like password entry, yes/no questions)
    to ensure only the calling thread can output to the terminal. Other threads'
    log messages will be buffered and flushed when the terminal is unlocked.




While locked:


    
  
  • The locking thread can use log_plain() to write to terminal
    • 
  
  • Other threads' log messages go to log file and are buffered
    • 
  
  • Buffered messages are flushed to terminal on unlock
    • 



Must be paired with log_unlock_terminal().




Returns


The previous terminal lock state (for nested calls)

792   bool expected = atomic_load_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked);
793   while (!atomic_cas_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked, &expected, true)) {
794     // Retry if CAS failed
795   }
796   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread, (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self());
797   return expected;
798 }

1120   }
1121   int msg_len = header_len;
1122   int formatted_len = safe_vsnprintf(log_buffer + header_len, sizeof(log_buffer) - (size_t)header_len, fmt, args);
1123   if (formatted_len < 0) {
1124     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format log message");
1125     va_end(args);
1126     return;
1127   }
1128 
1129   msg_len += formatted_len;
1130   // Truncate at whole line boundaries to avoid UTF-8 issues
1131   msg_len = truncate_at_whole_line(log_buffer, msg_len, sizeof(log_buffer));
1132   // Add newline if there's room and message doesn't already end with one
1133   if (msg_len > 0 && msg_len < (int)sizeof(log_buffer) - 1) {
1134     if (log_buffer[msg_len - 1] != '\n') {
1135       log_buffer[msg_len++] = '\n';
1136       log_buffer[msg_len] = '\0';
1137     }
1138   }
1139   va_end(args);
1140   // Validate UTF-8 in formatted message
1141   validate_log_message_utf8(log_buffer, "leveled log message");
1142 
1143   // Extract the user message part (without header) for JSON logging
1144   const char *user_message = log_buffer + header_len;
1145 
1146   // For JSON output: strip automatic trailing newline (keep explicit ones)
1147   char json_message_buf[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1148   const char *json_message = user_message;
1149   size_t user_msg_len = strlen(user_message);
1150 
1151   // If message ends with newline and it was added automatically (not in original message)
1152   if (user_msg_len > 0 && user_message[user_msg_len - 1] == '\n') {
1153     // The newline was added automatically if the message part didn't end with one
1154     // before we added it on line 1067. We can check this by looking at msg_len - 1
1155     // If msg_len >= header_len + 2, then msg_len - 2 would be the char before the newline
1156     int msg_content_len = msg_len - header_len;
1157     // NOLINTNEXTLINE(clang-analyzer-security.ArrayBound) - msg_content_len > 1 bounds check above
1158     if (msg_content_len > 1 && log_buffer[msg_len - 2] != '\n') {
1159       // Newline was added automatically - strip it for JSON
1160       if (user_msg_len - 1 < sizeof(json_message_buf)) {
1161         memcpy(json_message_buf, user_message, user_msg_len - 1);
1162         json_message_buf[user_msg_len - 1] = '\0';
1163         json_message = json_message_buf;
1164       }
1165     }
1166   }
1167 
1168   // Check if JSON format is enabled
1169   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
1170   bool json_format_enabled = (json_fd >= 0);
1171 
1172   // If JSON format is enabled, output ONLY JSON (skip text output)
1173   if (json_format_enabled) {
1174     // Output JSON to the JSON file descriptor
1175     log_json_write(json_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1176     // Also output JSON to console using unified routing logic, respecting quiet flag
1177     if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1178       int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1179       log_json_write(console_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1180     }
1181   } else {
1182     // Text format: output to file and terminal
1183     // No heap allocation - use log_buffer directly
1184     // Write to file (atomic write syscall) - use original buffer (with ANSI codes)
1185     int file_fd = (int)atomic_load_u64(&g_log.file);
1186     if (file_fd >= 0 && file_fd != STDERR_FILENO) {
1187       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1188     }
1189 
1190     // Write to terminal (atomic state checks)
1191     va_list args_terminal;
1192     va_start(args_terminal, fmt);
1193     write_to_terminal_atomic(level, time_buf, file, line, func, fmt, args_terminal, time_ns);
1194     va_end(args_terminal);
1195   }
1196 }

    colorize_log_message(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,

    LOG_COLOR_RESET, log_get_color_array(),
    log_json_write(), log_mmap_is_active(),

int
  log_named_format_message (const char * message, char * output, size_t
  output_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format hex addresses in a message as named object
  descriptions.


Parameters


message Input message (may contain hex addresses
  like 0x123456)


output Output buffer for formatted message


output_size Size of output buffer (must be > 0)


Returns


Length of output string (excluding null terminator) if
  any formatting was applied, -1 if no formatting was applied or on error


Scans the input message for hex addresses in the format
    0x[0-9a-fA-F]+. For each address found, checks the named object registry. If
    registered, replaces the address with "type: name (0xaddress)"
    format. If not registered, leaves the original hex address unchanged.




The output buffer is always null-terminated.




Note


The formatting is idempotent - applying it twice gives
  the same result



This function is efficient for messages with few hex addresses
    (common case)




Works correctly with 32-bit and 64-bit address formats




Definition at line 595 of file log/named.c.


595                                                                                     {
596   if (!message || !output || output_size == 0) {
597     return -1;
598   }
599 
600   size_t out_pos = 0;
601   const char *p = message;
602   bool any_transformed = false;
603 
604   while (*p && out_pos < output_size - 1) {
605     /* Look for "0x" prefix indicating a hex address */
606     if (*p == '0' && *(p + 1) == 'x' && isxdigit(*(p + 2))) {
607       const char *hex_start = p;
608       if (try_format_hex_address(message, hex_start, output, output_size, &out_pos, &p)) {
609         any_transformed = true;
610         continue;
611       }
612 
613       /* Not registered or formatting failed - copy original hex address */
614       const char *hex_end = hex_start + 2; /* Start after "0x" */
615       while (*hex_end && isxdigit(*hex_end)) {
616         hex_end++;
617       }
618       size_t hex_len = hex_end - hex_start;
619       if (hex_len < output_size - out_pos) {
620         memcpy(output + out_pos, hex_start, hex_len);
621         out_pos += hex_len;
622         p = hex_end;
623       } else {
624         /* Buffer overflow - truncate and stop */
625         break;
626       }
627     } else if (isdigit(*p)) {
628       /* Look for decimal integers that might be FDs or packet types */
629       const char *int_start = p;
630       int fd_value = 0;
631       int digit_count = 0;
632 
633       /* Parse decimal digits */
634       while (*p && isdigit(*p) && digit_count < 6) { /* FDs rarely exceed 999999 */
635         fd_value = fd_value * 10 + (*p - '0');
636         p++;
637         digit_count++;
638       }
639 
640       /* Skip if this integer is part of already-formatted output like "(fd=20)" or "(type/name (...))" */
641       bool is_already_formatted = false;
642       if (int_start - message >= 5) {
643         /* Check for "(fd=", "(pkt_type=", "(socket=", "(sockfd=", or "(type/" patterns to prevent re-formatting.
644          * Named objects are formatted as: type/name (0xaddress) or type/name (key=value)
645          * We need to detect if we're already inside a formatted output.
646          */
647         const char *check = int_start - 1;
648         while (check > message && isspace(*check))
649           check--;
650 
651         // Only access *check if pointer is within bounds
652         if (check >= message && *check == '=') {
653           /* Found "=", now check what prefix it has for patterns like "(fd=20)" or "(socket=20)" or "(pkt_type=123)" */
654           const char *eq_pos = check;
655           check--;
656           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
657             check--;
658           }
659           check++;
660 
661           size_t prefix_len = eq_pos - check;
662           if ((prefix_len == 2 && strncmp(check, "fd", 2) == 0) ||
663               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "socket", 6) == 0) ||
664               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "sockfd", 6) == 0) ||
665               (prefix_len == 8 && strncmp(check, "pkt_type", 8) == 0)) {
666             /* Only mark as already-formatted if inside formatted output:
667              * - "(" before keyword: parenthesized value like "(sockfd=15)"
668              * - "/" before keyword: name part of type/name like "fd/fd=7"
669              * Raw log patterns like "sockfd=15" should still be eligible for replacement. */
670             const char *before_kw = check - 1;
671             while (before_kw > message && isspace(*before_kw))
672               before_kw--;
673             if (before_kw >= message && (*before_kw == '(' || *before_kw == '/')) {
674               is_already_formatted = true;
675             }
676           }
677         } else if (check >= message && *check == '/') {
678           /* Found "/", now check if this is a type/name pattern.
679            * Format is "(type/name (...)" where type is word characters (thread, mutex, socket, etc.)
680            * Must verify "/" is preceded by word characters AND "(" to ensure it's a formatted type.
681            */
682           const char *slash_pos = check;
683           check--;
684           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
685             check--;
686           }
687           check++;
688 
689           /* Check that the "/" was preceded by word characters (indicating a type name) */
690           size_t type_len = slash_pos - check;
691           if (type_len > 0 && (isalpha(*check) || *check == '_')) {
692             /* Verify this is a registered type/name pattern by checking for "(" before the type
693              * AND checking if the type actually exists in the registry */
694             const char *type_start = check;
695             const char *before_type = type_start - 1;
696             while (before_type > message && isspace(*before_type)) {
697               before_type--;
698             }
699 
700             /* Only mark as formatted if:
701              * 1. Preceded by "(" - ensures pattern looks like "(type/name ...)"
702              * 2. Type exists in registry - prevents false positives on random text */
703             if (before_type >= message && *before_type == '(' && is_type_in_registry(type_start, type_len)) {
704               is_already_formatted = true;
705             }
706           }
707         }
708       }
709 
710       /* Check if this is a registered packet type */
711       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_packet_type_prefix(message, int_start)) {
712         const char *name = named_get_packet_type(fd_value);
713         if (name) {
714           const char *prefix_start = find_packet_type_prefix_start(message, int_start);
715           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
716           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
717 
718           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
719             out_pos -= prefix_len;
720           }
721 
722           if (write_formatted_packet_type(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
723             any_transformed = true;
724             continue;
725           }
726 
727           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
728         }
729       }
730 
731 
732       /* Check if this is a registered file descriptor */
733       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_fd_prefix(message, int_start)) {
734         const char *name = named_get_fd(fd_value);
735         const char *reg_type = NULL;
736 
737         /* Fallback: check main registry for sockets registered via NAMED_REGISTER_SOCKET
738          * (which uses raw integer key, not FD namespace encoding) */
739         if (!name) {
740           name = named_get((uintptr_t)(intptr_t)fd_value);
741           if (name) {
742             reg_type = named_get_type((uintptr_t)(intptr_t)fd_value);
743           }
744         }
745 
746         if (name) {
747           const char *prefix_start = find_fd_prefix_start(message, int_start);
748           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
749           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
750 
751           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
752             out_pos -= prefix_len;
753           }
754 
755           bool formatted = false;
756           if (reg_type) {
757             /* From main registry — use registered type and original prefix keyword */
758             const char *kw_end = prefix_start;
759             while (kw_end < int_start && (isalnum(*kw_end) || *kw_end == '_'))
760               kw_end++;
761             size_t kw_len = kw_end - prefix_start;
762 
763             char kw_buf[32];
764             if (kw_len > 0 && kw_len < sizeof(kw_buf)) {
765               memcpy(kw_buf, prefix_start, kw_len);
766               kw_buf[kw_len] = '\0';
767             } else {
768               snprintf(kw_buf, sizeof(kw_buf), "%s", reg_type);
769             }
770 
771             char temp[512];
772             int written = snprintf(temp, sizeof(temp), "%s/%s (%s=%d)", reg_type, name, kw_buf, fd_value);
773             if (written > 0 && (size_t)written < sizeof(temp) && out_pos + (size_t)written < output_size - 1) {
774               memcpy(output + out_pos, temp, (size_t)written);
775               out_pos += (size_t)written;
776               formatted = true;
777             }
778           } else {
779             formatted = write_formatted_fd(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos);
780           }
781 
782           if (formatted) {
783             any_transformed = true;
784             continue;
785           }
786 
787           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
788         }
789       }
790 
791       /* Check if this integer has a generic type prefix (socket, client, connection, etc.)
792        * DISABLED: This feature has a backtracking bug that corrupts output when applied after
793        * other formatting operations. Re-enable only after fixing the backtracking logic to
794        * properly track correspondence between input and output positions.
795        * See commit b86fed2a8 which introduced the bug.
796        */
797       // NOLINTNEXTLINE(readability-simplify-boolean-expr) - Intentionally disabled code, see comment above
798       if (false && !is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0) {
799         const char *type_name = NULL;
800         size_t type_len = 0;
801         const char *prefix_start = find_generic_type_prefix(message, int_start, &type_name, &type_len);
802 
803         if (type_name && prefix_start != int_start) {
804           /* Skip if already formatted with a name (e.g., "socket/listener.0" already has a /) */
805           bool skip_format = false;
806           /* Check if this looks like it's already been formatted by looking for / in the pattern */
807           const char *check_ptr = prefix_start;
808           while (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr && *check_ptr != '/') {
809             check_ptr++;
810           }
811           if (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr == '/') {
812             /* Already formatted (contains /), skip */
813             skip_format = true;
814           }
815 
816           if (!skip_format) {
817             /* We found a type prefix like "socket", "client", etc.
818              * Try to look up a registered name for this type+id */
819 
820             /* Normalize type name aliases: "sockfd" → "socket", "descriptor" → "fd" */
821             const char *lookup_type = type_name;
822             size_t lookup_len = type_len;
823             if (type_len == 6 && strncmp(type_name, "sockfd", 6) == 0) {
824               lookup_type = "socket";
825               lookup_len = 6;
826             } else if (type_len == 10 && strncmp(type_name, "descriptor", 10) == 0) {
827               lookup_type = "fd";
828               lookup_len = 2;
829             }
830 
831             const char *name = named_get_by_type_and_id(lookup_type, lookup_len, fd_value);
832 
833             char temp_output[512];
834             char type_str[32];
835             char id_buffer[64];
836 
837             /* Safely copy type name */
838             if (type_len < sizeof(type_str)) {
839               memcpy(type_str, type_name, type_len);
840               type_str[type_len] = '\0';
841 
842               int id_written = snprintf(id_buffer, sizeof(id_buffer), "%d", fd_value);
843               if (id_written > 0 && id_written < (int)sizeof(id_buffer)) {
844                 int temp_written = 0;
845                 if (name) {
846                   /* Format with name: type/name (type=value) */
847                   temp_written =
848                       snprintf(temp_output, sizeof(temp_output), "%s/%s (%s=%s)", type_str, name, type_str, id_buffer);
849 
850                   if (temp_written > 0 && (size_t)temp_written < sizeof(temp_output)) {
851                     /* Backtrack to remove the prefix we already copied */
852                     size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
853                     if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
854                       out_pos -= prefix_len;
855                     }
856 
857                     int copy_len = temp_written;
858                     if (out_pos + copy_len < output_size - 1) {
859                       memcpy(output + out_pos, temp_output, copy_len);
860                       out_pos += copy_len;
861                       any_transformed = true;
862                       continue;
863                     }
864                   }
865                 }
866               }
867             }
868           }
869         }
870       }
871 
872       /* Not a registered FD or formatting failed - copy original number */
873       if (!copy_unformatted_decimal(int_start, p, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
874         break;
875       }
876     } else {
877       /* Check if this is the start of "type=DIGIT" pattern */
878       if (*p == 't' && p + 4 < message + strlen(message) && is_type_equals_pattern(p)) {
879         if (!type_equals_inside_parens(p, message)) {
880           const char *digit_end = p + 5;
881           int pkt_value = parse_decimal_digits(digit_end, &digit_end);
882 
883           if (pkt_value > 0) {
884             const char *name = named_get_packet_type(pkt_value);
885             if (name && write_formatted_type_equals(pkt_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
886               p = digit_end;
887               any_transformed = true;
888               continue;
889             }
890           }
891         }
892       }
893 
894       /* Regular character - copy it */
895       copy_char_to_output(output, &out_pos, *p++);
896     }
897   }
898 
899   /* Null terminate */
900   if (out_pos < output_size) {
901     output[out_pos] = '\0';
902   } else {
903     output[output_size - 1] = '\0';
904   }
905 
906   return any_transformed ? (int)out_pos : -1;
907 }

Referenced by log_named_format_or_original().






const
  char * log_named_format_or_original (const char * message)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format named objects in a message (thread-local buffer).


Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string if any formatting was applied,
  original message if no formatting was applied


Convenience wrapper that handles buffer management internally
    using a thread-local buffer. The returned pointer is valid only until the
    next call to this function from the same thread.




Safe to use directly in log macros:




log_info("State: %s", log_named_format_or_original(msg));



Note


Maximum formatted message length is 4096 bytes



If format buffer overflows, returns original message




Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string (thread-local buffer, valid only
  until next call)


Convenience wrapper that uses a thread-local buffer internally.
    Applies formatting iteratively until the message stops changing (fixpoint).
    Returns the original message if no formatting was applied.


Definition at line 918 of file log/named.c.


918                                                               {
919   if (!message) {
920     return message;
921   }
922 
923   static _Thread_local char format_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
924   static _Thread_local char work_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
925 
926   /* Apply formatting iteratively until the message stabilizes (no more changes) */
927   const char *current = message;
928   size_t max_iterations = 3; /* Reduce iterations to prevent recursion (prevent transforming already-transformed output) */
929 
930   for (size_t iter = 0; iter < max_iterations; iter++) {
931     int result = log_named_format_message(current, format_buffer, sizeof(format_buffer));
932 
933     if (result <= 0) {
934       /* No transformation was made, we've reached a fixpoint */
935       return current == message ? message : format_buffer;
936     }
937 
938     /* Check if the message changed */
939     if (strcmp(current, format_buffer) == 0) {
940       /* No actual change, return the current version */
941       return format_buffer;
942     }
943 
944     /* Message changed, prepare for next iteration */
945     if (current == message) {
946       /* First iteration, use format_buffer as input for next iteration */
947       current = format_buffer;
948     } else {
949       /* Subsequent iterations, swap buffers */
950       asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer), format_buffer);
951       if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
952         log_error("Failed to copy format buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
953         return format_buffer;
954       }
955       current = work_buffer;
956     }
957   }
958 
959   /* Max iterations reached, return last formatted version */
960   return format_buffer;
961 }

    log_named_format_message(), NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE, and
    SAFE_STRCPY.

1239   msg_len = truncate_at_whole_line(log_buffer, msg_len, sizeof(log_buffer));
1240 
1241   // Validate UTF-8 in formatted message
1242   validate_log_message_utf8(log_buffer, "plain text log");
1243 
1244   // Write to mmap if active
1245   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
1246     log_mmap_write(LOG_INFO, NULL, 0, NULL, "%s", log_buffer);
1247   } else {
1248     // Write to file with headers (atomic write syscall)
1249     int file_fd = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.file);
1250     if (file_fd >= 0 && file_fd != STDERR_FILENO) {
1251       // Add header with timestamp and log level to file output
1252       char time_buf[LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE];
1253       uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1254       get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);
1255 
1256       char header_buffer[512];
1257       int header_len = format_log_header(header_buffer, sizeof(header_buffer), LOG_INFO, time_buf, "lib/log/logging.c",
1258                                          0, "log_plain_msg", false, time_ns);
1259 
1260       if (header_len > 0) {
1261         write_to_log_file_atomic(header_buffer, header_len, NULL);
1262       }
1263       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1264       write_to_log_file_atomic("\n", 1, NULL);
1265     }
1266   }
1267 
1268   // Terminal output (atomic state checks)
1269   if (!atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1270     return;
1271   }
1272   if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_locked)) {
1273     uint64_t owner = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread);
1274     if (owner != (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self()) {
1275       return;
1276     }
1277   }
1278 
1279   // Check if JSON format is enabled
1280   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
1281   bool json_format_enabled = (json_fd >= 0);
1282 
1283   if (json_format_enabled) {
1284     // Output JSON for plain messages too
1285     uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1286     int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1287     log_json_write(console_fd, LOG_INFO, time_ns, __FILE__, __LINE__, "log_plain_msg", log_buffer);
1288   } else {
1289     // Choose output stream using unified routing logic (LOG_INFO level)
1290     int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1291     FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1292 
1293     // Apply colorization for TTY output
1294     if (terminal_should_color_output(fd)) {
1295       const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(log_buffer);
1296       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", colorized_msg);
1297     } else {
1298       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", log_buffer);
1299     }
1300     (void)fflush(output_stream);
1301   }
1302 }






References args, asciichat_thread_self,
    atomic_load_int(), atomic_load_u64(),
    colorize_log_message(), get_current_time_formatted(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), LOG_INFO, log_json_write(),
    log_mmap_is_active(), log_mmap_write(),
    LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE, LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE,
    safe_fprintf(), safe_vsnprintf(),
    shutdown_is_requested(), terminal_choose_log_fd(),
    terminal_should_color_output(), and
  time_get_realtime_ns().

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Converts a plain text log line (from log file) into a colored version
    matching the format used for terminal output. Applies colors to:


    
  
  • Timestamp and level based on log level
    • 
  
  • Thread ID in grey
    • 
  
  • File path in cyan
    • 
  
  • Line number in magenta
    • 
  
  • Function name in orange/DEV color
    • 
  
  • Message body colorized appropriately
    • 



Expected plain format (debug mode): [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL]
    [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE




Parameters


plain_line Plain text log line (from log file)


colored_buf Output buffer for colored version (must be large enough)


buf_size Size of colored_buf


Returns


Length of colored string, 0 on error or invalid
  format


Note


Uses static buffer internally, result is reused across
  calls


Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID]
    FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes
    matching the colored format.


Definition at line 1881 of file log/log.c.


1881                                                                                            {
1882   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1883     return 0;
1884   }
1885 
1886   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1887 
1888   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1889   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1890   const char *p = plain_line;
1891 
1892   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1893   if (*p != '[') {
1894     return 0; // Invalid format
1895   }
1896   p++;
1897   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1898 
1899   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1900   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1901   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1902     p++;
1903   }
1904   if (*p != ']') {
1905     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1906   }
1907 
1908   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1909   char timestamp[64];
1910   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1911     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1912   }
1913   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1914   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1915   p++; // Skip ]
1916 
1917   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1918   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1919     p++;
1920   }
1921 
1922   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1923   if (*p != '[') {
1924     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1925   }
1926   p++;
1927   const char *level_start = p;
1928   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1929     p++;
1930   }
1931   if (*p != ']') {
1932     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1933   }
1934   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1935   char level_str[16];
1936   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1937     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1938   }
1939   memcpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1940   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1941 
1942   // Determine log level for color selection
1943   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1944   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1945     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1946   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1947     level = LOG_INFO;
1948   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1949     level = LOG_WARN;
1950   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1951     level = LOG_ERROR;
1952   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1953     level = LOG_FATAL;
1954   }
1955 
1956   p++; // Skip ]
1957 
1958   // Skip whitespace after level
1959   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1960     p++;
1961   }
1962 
1963   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1964   uint64_t tid = 0;
1965   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1966     p += 5;
1967     char *tid_end = NULL;
1968     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1969     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1970       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1971       // Try to find the closing bracket
1972       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1973         p++;
1974       }
1975       if (*p == ']') {
1976         p++;
1977       }
1978     } else {
1979       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1980     }
1981     // Skip whitespace after tid
1982     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1983       p++;
1984     }
1985   }
1986   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1987 
1988   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1989   const char *file_start = p;
1990   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1991     p++;
1992   }
1993   if (*p != ':') {
1994     return 0; // Malformed
1995   }
1996   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1997   char file_path[256];
1998   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1999     return 0;
2000   }
2001   memcpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
2002   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
2003   p++; // Skip :
2004 
2005   // Extract line number (digits only)
2006   int line_num = 0;
2007   const char *line_start = p;
2008   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
2009     p++;
2010   }
2011   if (p == line_start) {
2012     return 0; // No line number
2013   }
2014   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
2015 
2016   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
2017   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
2018     p++;
2019   }
2020 
2021   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
2022   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
2023     p += 3; // Skip "in "
2024   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
2025     // Allow tab after "in"
2026     p += 2; // Skip "in"
2027     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
2028       p++;
2029     }
2030   } else {
2031     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
2032     return 0;
2033   }
2034 
2035   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
2036   const char *func_start = p;
2037   while (*p && *p != '(') {
2038     p++;
2039   }
2040   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
2041     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
2042   }
2043   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
2044 
2045   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
2046   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
2047     func_len--;
2048   }
2049 
2050   char func_name[256];
2051   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
2052     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
2053   }
2054   if (func_len > 0) {
2055     memcpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
2056   }
2057   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
2058 
2059   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
2060   if (*p == '(') {
2061     p++;
2062     while (*p && *p != ')') {
2063       p++;
2064     }
2065     if (*p == ')') {
2066       p++;
2067     }
2068   }
2069 
2070   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
2071   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
2072     p++;
2073   }
2074 
2075   // Remaining is the message
2076   const char *message = p;
2077 
2078   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
2079   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
2080   if (!colors) {
2081     // No colors available, return plain text
2082     static bool warned_once = false;
2083     if (!warned_once) {
2084       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
2085       warned_once = true;
2086     }
2087     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2088     if (len >= buf_size) {
2089       return 0;
2090     }
2091     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2092     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2093     return len;
2094   }
2095 
2096   // Build colored output
2097   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2098   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2099   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2100   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2101   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2102   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2103 
2104   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2105                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2106                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2107                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2108 
2109   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2110     return 0;
2111   }
2112 
2113   // Colorize the message part
2114   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2115   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2116                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2117                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2118                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2119 
2120   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2121     return 0;
2122   }
2123 
2124   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2125   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2126   return (size_t)len;
2127 }

  log_redetect_terminal_capabilities (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Re-detect terminal capabilities after logging is initialized.

1607 
1608   // Guard against recursion
1609   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1610     return;
1611   }
1612 
1613 
1614   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1615   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1616   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1617   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
1618     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = true;
1619     g_log.terminal_caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1620     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1621     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1622 
1623 
1624     // Now log the capabilities AFTER colors are set, so this log uses the correct colors
1625     log_debug("Terminal capabilities: color_level=%d, capabilities=0x%x, utf8=%s, fps=%d",
1626               g_log.terminal_caps.color_level, g_log.terminal_caps.capabilities,
1627               g_log.terminal_caps.utf8_support ? "yes" : "no", g_log.terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1628 
1629     // Now that we've detected once with reliable results, keep these colors consistent for all future logs
1630   } else {
1631   }
1632   // Once initialized with reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent

void

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Force all terminal log output to stderr.


Parameters


enabled true to force all logs to
  stderr, false for normal routing


When enabled, all log messages (including INFO, DEBUG, DEV) go to
    stderr instead of the default behavior where INFO/DEBUG/DEV go to stdout and
    WARN/ERROR/FATAL go to stderr. This is used by the client to keep stdout
    clean for ASCII art output.

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Set a custom log format string.


Parameters


format_str Format string with specifiers like
  time(H:M:S), level, message, etc. Pass NULL to use default format. Empty
  string "" also uses default.

748                                                                             {
749   /* Free old format if it exists */
750   if (g_log.format) {
751     log_template_free(g_log.format);
752     g_log.format = NULL;
753   }
754   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
755     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
756     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
757   }
758 
759   /* Use default format if NULL or empty string */
760   const char *format_to_use = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0') ? format_str : OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT;
761   bool is_custom = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0');
762 
763   /* Parse the format string (always parse, never skip) */
764   log_template_t *parsed_format = log_template_parse(format_to_use, false);
765 
766   if (!parsed_format) {
767     log_error("Failed to parse log format: %s", format_to_use);
768     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid log format string");
769   }
770 
771   /* If console_only is true, we also need the default format for file output */
772   if (console_only && is_custom) {
773     log_template_t *default_format = log_template_parse(OPT_LOG_TEMPLATE_DEFAULT, false);
774 
775     if (!default_format) {
776       log_template_free(parsed_format);
777       log_error("Failed to parse default log format");
778       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to parse default format");
779     }
780     g_log.format = default_format;
781     g_log.format_console_only = parsed_format;
782   } else {
783     g_log.format = parsed_format;
784     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
785   }
786 
787   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.has_custom_format, is_custom);
788   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
789 }

704                                            {
705   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
706   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
707 
708   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
709   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
710   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
711   if (enabled) {
712     const options_t *opts = options_get();
713     if (opts && opts->quiet) {
714       return; // Silently ignore attempts to enable terminal output when --quiet is set

void
  log_shutdown_begin (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Begin shutdown phase - disable console logging but keep file
    logging. Call this before logging shutdown messages. Disables console output
    but keeps file logging so messages are recorded for debugging.




Useful when you want final messages (like "no servers
    found") to go to the log file only, not to stdout where it might
    interfere with output.

1812   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, false);
1813   g_log.shutdown_in_progress = true;

void
  log_system_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Shutdown the logging system internal state (system-level cleanup).
    Restores terminal state and clears registered session log buffer. Must be
    called once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).




Clears registered session log buffer. Color cleanup is handled
    separately in asciichat_shared_destroy() after memory reporting. Must
    be called once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call
    multiple times (idempotent).
